evidence: In October 2025, we entered into a product purchase agreement with OpenAI OpCo, LLC, (OpenAI) to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs, with the deployment of the first gigawatt of capacity powered by our AMD Instinct MI450 series products.

We strengthened our AI leadership through a number of strategic acquisitions during the year... acquired ZT Group Int l, Inc. (ZT Systems)... to deliver end-to-end AI solutions and accelerate the design and deployment of AMD-powered AI infrastructure.
We continue to invest in software capabilities and the open ecosystem through the AMD ROCm platform, delivering new features for high-performance AI training and inference.
Demand for our data center AI accelerator products was strong as large hyperscale customers, OEMs and ODMs deployed our AMD Instinct MI350X Series GPUs.

In October 2025, we entered into a product purchase agreement with OpenAI... to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs, with the deployment of the first gigawatt of capacity powered by our AMD Instinct MI450 series products.

We launched AMD Ryzen AI 300 Series processors featuring a next generation NPU supporting Microsoft Copilot+ PCs... In January 2026, we launched our Ryzen AI 400 series processors, the next generation of processors for AI PCs.
Issues related to the responsible use of AI may result in reputational, competitive and financial harm and liability.

Nvidia Corporation (Nvidia) leverages its market position in data center GPU, financial resources, and proprietary software ecosystem to promote its systems and influences customers who do business with us.
We may encounter competition from customers who internally develop products to support similar AI workloads to those supported by ours.

capex_mentions.context: Multiyear strategic partnership with OpenAI for GPU deployment, October 2025

capex_mentions.quote: acquired ZT Group Int l, Inc. (ZT Systems)... retained certain intellectual property and employees associated with the design operations
capex_mentions.context: Strategic acquisition in March 2025 to enable end-to-end AI infrastructure solutions

capex_mentions.quote: We made other strategic acquisitions during the year to advance our software capabilities... support co-packaged optics solutions for next-generation AI systems, and bring deep expertise in high-speed inference and reasoning-based AI technologies

limitations: No specific R&D dollar amounts or total capex figures for AI were disclosed in the excerpts; the 6-gigawatt OpenAI agreement is a product purchase/deployment commitment rather than a traditional capex figure, but represents a quantified, large-scale AI commitment.

Risk disclosure depth scored 4 based on named competitive mechanisms (Nvidia ecosystem lock-in, customer self-development of AI chips) and reputational/financial harm from AI misuse, but no quantified risk exposure or named internal AI governance body was found in the excerpts.
Excerpts are pre-filtered; additional financial detail (e.g., R&D spend breakdown, total capex) may exist in MD&A or financial statement sections not included here.
